game_name: Super Fire Pro Wrestling X Premium

danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite animation changes, knockdown states, visual impact effects
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent is taking damage and losing match advantage

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental changes in hardcore match types (cage appearance, barbed wire visuals, electrical effects)
danger_signals.meaning: Match conditions have become more dangerous; standard wrestling rules suspended

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent in downed or stunned animation state
danger_signals.meaning: Player has opportunity to execute damaging move; window-based danger for opponent

safety_signals.visual_form: Player character standing, opponent knocked down or in recovery animation
safety_signals.meaning: Player has tactical advantage and can dictate match pace

safety_signals.visual_form: Referee visible in ring during standard match types
safety_signals.meaning: Match rules are being enforced; extreme tactics penalized

safety_signals.visual_form: Opponent rising from canvas, regaining stance
safety_signals.meaning: Opponent is recovering; window of vulnerability closing

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Character move-set and wrestler likenesses differentiate style; strong-style wrestlers use different animation sets than sports-entertainment wrestlers
High-flying move animations, mask designs, agility-focused sprite behavior differentiate from other styles
Stiff strike animations, submission hold emphasis, realistic damage accumulation in CPU behavior
Brawling animations, use of foreign objects, high-impact move sets visually distinct from technical wrestlers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Match environment includes hazards (cages, barbed wire, electrical effects)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ring with standard ropes and padded corners; referee present in neutral position; clean canvas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Wrestlers follow technical grappling rules; referee enforces regulations; count-outs and disqualifications possible

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Hardcore Match (Landmine Death Match, Electric Barbed Wire Cage)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ring enclosed or modified with visible hazards; cage structures, barbed wire, or electrical effects rendered; referee may be absent or non-intervening
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Extreme moves enabled; standard rules suspended; wrestlers use environment as weapon; no disqualifications

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two wrestlers per team positioned at corners; tag rope visible; corner posts may be color-coded or otherwise distinguished
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Tag mechanics enforced; legal wrestler restricted; partners wait outside ring; tag rules affect match flow

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Different ring/arena setup; combat-specific visual styling
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat sports rules override wrestling rules; move-sets emphasize striking or alternative grappling

environmental_storytelling.example: Real-life wrestlers represented under different names due to licensing constraints; visual character design communicates wrestler identity to knowledgeable players despite name changes
Ring attire, costume design, and physical characteristics convey wrestler character and promotion affiliation through visual design rather than explicit labels
Hardcore match environments (cages, barbed wire, electrical hazards) communicate that standard wrestling rules have been abandoned without explicit UI notification
Signature move animations and wrestling style visual differences communicate wrestler nationality/promotion style (lucha-libre high-flying vs. Japanese strong-style stiff strikes)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Aggressive move execution, varied move-set usage, strategic positioning
Limited move-set, reactive rather than initiative-taking, extended recovery times, defensive posturing
Desperation moves, high-risk tactics, animation sequences indicating fatigue
Visible in ring, enforces rules, counts knockdowns, initiates disqualifications
Removed from match area or non-intervening; rule enforcement disabled

notes: Super Fire Pro Wrestling X Premium (1996) communicates world-state primarily through match-type environmental design and wrestler behavioral/aesthetic differentiation rather than explicit UI elements. The game's political/factional landscape is represented implicitly through wrestling style variations (North American, Mexican, Japanese strong-style, hardcore) without explicit faction labeling. Danger and match control are communicated through sprite animation states (knockdown, recovery, stunning), opponent positioning, and CPU behavior intensity. The absence of licensed promotion names creates a 'nameless world' where visual design and move-set become the primary communication vectors for character identity and wrestling style affiliation. Wikipedia source provided limited specific visual detail for SNES iteration; analysis based on series-level documentation of Fire Pro Wrestling design philosophy applied to this title.
sources_used: wikipedia - Fire Pro Wrestling (series overview, design features, match types, edit mode documentation)
